reasoning:
The forecast horizon is extremely short (barely over a month from the May 29, 2025 cutoff to the July 1, 2025 resolution date). To resolve YES, Senator Fetterman would have to resign, be expelled, or explicitly leave the Democratic caucus in the next 30 days. While he has faced significant pushback following a highly critical New York Magazine profile in early May, and progressive groups like Indivisible PA have called for his resignation, Fetterman has robustly denied the allegations and repeatedly vowed to finish his six-year term. He has also previously strongly rejected rumors of switching parties or becoming an Independent, calling the idea a 'rocket sled to Palookaville.' 

Given Fetterman's known defiant posture toward his progressive critics, it is highly out of character for him to cave to their pressure and resign. The base rate for a Senator resigning or leaving their caucus within a one-month window is negligible absent a terminal health diagnosis or a formal federal indictment resulting in uniform leadership pressure. While Fetterman does have a history of serious health struggles, he continues to assert he is medically fine. The only remaining paths to YES are a sudden and catastrophic health turn or an unprecedented overnight collapse of his relationship with the caucus, both of which are highly improbable in effectively 30 days.
